codeReview.summary: Reviewed the mission docs, feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, worker entrypoint, and health-route tests. The feature's code change is narrowly scoped to the required Cloudflare compatibility fix in apps/browser-executor/wrangler.toml, and the deployment evidence is consistent: the worker initially failed on node:buffer, succeeded after adding nodejs_compat, and the deployed POST /health endpoint returned {"status":"ok"} with HTTP 200. I found no high-confidence blocking or non-blocking defects within the reviewed feature scope for VAL-BWIRE-006.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The cf-worker skill is too rigid for deployment-only Cloudflare tasks: it requires local wrangler dev verification and says wrangler.toml should not be modified, but this feature needed a wrangler.toml compatibility flag change and direct verification against the deployed workers.dev endpoint.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/cf-worker/SKILL.md:40 requires `cd apps/browser-executor && bunx wrangler dev --port 8787`, and :105 says `wrangler.toml` already has correct bindings — do NOT modify. The reviewed handoff at /Users/alvaro.ybanez/.factory/missions/6ceae915-bba1-41a1-9dec-4ed54e3c81f0/handoffs/2026-03-31T11-51-41-572Z__deploy-browser-executor__f6854d7b-873b-4f4a-b4c8-05874ad5f75c.json:21-28 shows deployment failed with `No such module "node:buffer"` until `compatibility_flags = ["nodejs_compat"]` was added, and :70-79 records the justified deviation to remote deploy + public health-check verification.

summary: Pass. The reviewed feature added the required Cloudflare node compatibility flag, the deployment evidence shows secrets were set and the worker deployed successfully, and both the recorded evidence and an independent review-time POST to /health returned HTTP 200 with `{"status":"ok"}`.
